channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Politics Friday: Crime, public safety measures drive debates at the Capitol
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about new public safety bills and criminal justice debates at the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Senate Republicans approve budget framework, pushing past Democratic objections after all-night vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican senators pushed a $340 billion budget framework to passage early Friday, chugging through an all-night session and Democratic opposition in a step toward unleashing money the Trump administration says it needs for mass deportations and border security that top their agen
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats channel their outrage over DOGE, Ukraine and more in marathon Senate session
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The budget resolution from Republicans was on the agenda in the Senate, but the late-night debate encompassed so much more.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge allows Trump's mass firings of federal workers to move forward
item.description: A federal judge in Washington has allowed President Donald Trump’s mass firings of federal workers to move forward.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gov. Hochul won't remove NYC Mayor Eric Adams, but pushes to limit powers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams will remain in office as Gov. Kathy Hochul pushes to implement "certain guardrails" to help protect New Yorkers. Hochul cited the “democratic process” as why she chose not to remove Adams from office.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Justice Department broadens Jan. 6 pardons to cover gun, drug-related charges
item.description: In a new development, the Department of Justice said Trump's pardon of Jan. 6 defendants should apply more broadly and include separate gun charges, as well.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: Trump holds Black History Month event as some agencies skip recognition after anti-DEI order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump marked Black History Month at the White House on Thursday by making an appearance at a celebratory reception with a surprise guest, golf legend Tiger Woods, while calling out other athletes in attendance and marveling at the size of the crowd.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Trump's mass layoffs raise the risk of wildfires in the US West, according to fired workers
item.description: The termination letters that ended the careers of thousands of U.S. Forest Service employees mean fewer people and less resources will be available to help prevent and fight wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle And Claire Rush, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's DOGE stays on track after pair of federal judge rulings
item.description: A pair of federal judges, in two separate cases, filed rulings that allow DOGE to continue its track of finding government waste and slashing it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says this congressman would have his 'Total Endorsement' if he runs for Florida governor
item.description: President Donald Trump is making it very clear he would endorse Republican Rep. Byron Donalds if he runs in the 2026 race to succeed term-limited Florida Gov.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk waves a chainsaw and charms conservatives talking up Trump's cost-cutting efforts
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk has created a buzz waving a chainsaw in the air at a conservative gathering outside Washington.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: First CDC vaccine meeting under Trump administration is postponed
item.description: The panel of vaccine experts were supposed to hold their first meeting under the Trump administration in late February. It’s not clear when that meeting will now take place.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Judge won't immediately block Trump administration’s abrupt halt to Catholic refugee funding
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday refused to immediately block the Trump administration’s abrupt halt to funding of the nation’s largest private refugee resettlement program in a setback to the U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration throws out protections from deportation for roughly half a million Haitians
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is throwing out protections that shielded roughly half a million Haitians from deportation, meaning they would lose their work permits and could be eligible to be removed from the country by August.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ellison: Trump order on transgender athletes violates Minnesota law
item.description: Attorney General Keith Ellison’s opinion comes days after the Trump administration said it would investigate the state high school league’s plan to follow state law and let students participate in sports consistent with their gender identity, despite the president’s executive order.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Dems to force votes on Medicaid as it becomes sore point in Senate budget fight
item.description: Democrats are planning to make Republicans take uncomfortable amendment votes, including several on protecting Medicaid from cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US official says Trump's frustration with Zelenskyy is 'multifold' and blasts 'insults' from Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top White House official said Thursday that President Donald Trump’s increasingly tough criticism of Volodymyr Zelenskyy reflects the administration’s growing frustration with what they see as the Ukrainian leader creating roadblocks to finding an endgame to Russia’s invasion of
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With insults and bravado, Trump takes campaign messaging into White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump won reelection months ago and is barred from seeking another term. You might not know it from official White House communications.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Kentucky AG who handled Breonna Taylor probe promptly announces Senate bid to replace Mitch McConnell
item.description: Former Kentucky Attorney General Daniel Cameron is running to replace Sen. Mitch McConnell, the Republican leader who is not seeking re-election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk makes surprise appearance at CPAC
item.description: Elon Musk is set to deliver his debut speech at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A federal judge has denied states‘ bid to halt DOGE and Musk‘s work
item.description: A federal judge has denied a bid to temporarily halt DOGE from its controversial work at certain federal agencies. Meanwhile, the White House says Elon Musk is not technically DOGE’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay sheriffs to advise Florida cabinet on immigration enforcement
item.description: Two Tampa Bay area sheriffs were tapped to join the newly created State Immigration Enforcement Council on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
